fact: September 7 is exactly 250 days into the year - that's more than 2/3 of the whole year gone!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when monarch butterflies are typically making their epic 3,000-mile journey to Mexico!
The sun is setting about 2-3 minutes earlier each day around September 7, which means darkness is sneaking up on us faster than a ninja!
September 7 falls in Virgo season - the zodiac sign known for being super organized and probably color-coding their sock drawers.
In many places, this is peak spider season when eight-legged architects are building their most impressive webs to catch flying snacks.
Tree leaves in cooler regions are starting their secret chemical shutdown, slowly draining the green chlorophyll that makes them look alive.
September 7 is only about 2 weeks away from the autumn equinox, when day and night duke it out for exactly 12 hours each!
In the Southern Hemisphere, September 7 is like nature hitting the fast-forward button on spring - flowers are popping up everywhere!
The morning dew gets extra thick around this time in many places because the air temperature drops faster than the ground temperature at night.
September 7 marks the time when many birds' internal GPS systems start going bonkers, telling them it's time to fly thousands of miles south.
Squirrels are in full-blown panic mode by September 7, frantically burying nuts like tiny furry doomsday preppers.
